key_insight: PS-PPO reduces RLHF training cost by backpropagating only through a sampled prefix of each trajectory while using importance weighting to keep the gradient estimator unbiased.
engineering_relevance: This makes critic-free RLHF more practical for long reasoning traces by lowering compute and peak GPU memory without changing the full-trajectory objective.
method: Sample a prompt-conditioned cutoff timestep for each rollout.
Backpropagate only through the sampled prefix instead of the full trajectory.
Apply importance-weighting correction to preserve unbiased learning signals.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experiments on mathematical reasoning and RLHF benchmarks, showing large reductions in training compute and peak GPU memory while keeping accuracy comparable to strong critic-free baselines.
watch_item: The method depends on a cutoff-sampling and importance-weighting design, so poor cutoff distributions or high-variance weights could hurt stability.
paper_title: PS-PPO: Prefix-Sampling PPO for Critic-Free RLHF
